title: Completed comprehensive research on demulcent herbs for tickly cough. Created demulcent-herbs-tic...
content:
Completed comprehensive research on demulcent herbs for tickly cough. Created demulcent-herbs-tickly-cough-research.md with deep dive into marshmallow root, slippery elm, and licorice root.

KEY FINDINGS:
- Marshmallow root is the clear winner: fast-acting, sustainable, safe, easy to prepare
- Cold infusion is critical for marshmallow - extracts pure mucilage vs hot water extracting starches
- Coating effect lasts 2-4 hours, requiring frequent dosing (3-6x daily for acute cough)
- Slippery elm has serious sustainability concerns (Dutch Elm Disease, no cultivation) - should be avoided
- Licorice has significant safety issues - as little as 100mg glycyrrhizic acid raises blood pressure

PRACTICAL PROTOCOLS DEVELOPED:
- Acute cough protocol (marshmallow cold infusion every 2-3 hours)
- Preventive/early intervention protocol
- Nighttime-specific strategy
- Travel-friendly preparations
- Children's protocols
- When to add expectorants

PREPARATION METHODS:
- Marshmallow cold infusion (overnight soak - most effective)
- Marshmallow-honey syrup (concentrated, travel-friendly)
- Slippery elm gruel technique (paste first to avoid clumps)
- Proper licorice decoction (max 5 min boil, remove bark)

All sources cited from EMA, Commission E, PubMed, King's American Dispensatory, practitioner sites.

Document is ready for integration into folkprotocol materia/protocol content.
